#topbar-cta-button a {
  background-color: #141414 !important;
  color: #ffffff !important;
  padding: 0.5rem 1.25rem !important;
  border-radius: 8px;
  font-weight: 600;
  transition: opacity 0.15s ease;
}

#topbar-cta-button a:hover {
  opacity: 0.85 !important;
}

:is(.dark, [data-theme='dark']) #topbar-cta-button a,
:is(.dark, [data-theme='dark']) #topbar-cta-button a span {
  background-color: #ffffff !important;
  color: #141414 !important;
}

/* Center the tab links */
.nav-tabs {
  justify-content: center;
  flex: 1;
}

/*
 * HACK: Hide the "Overview" sidebar group header in the Developers tab.
 *
 * Mintlify requires every page to be inside a named group, and the tab's
 * landing page is always the first page of the first group. We want the
 * Developer Overview page to be the tab landing without showing a lonely
 * "Overview" group header above a single item.
 *
 * We use :has() to scope the rule to only the sidebar group that contains
 * the developers/introduction link, so other tabs are unaffected.
 */
div:has(> .sidebar-group a[href='/developers/introduction']),
div:has(> .sidebar-group a[href='/user-guide/introduction']) {
  display: none;
}

/* Remove the top margin on the group that follows the hidden overview group,
   so it sits flush at the top of the sidebar without a gap. */
div:has(> .sidebar-group a[href='/developers/introduction']) + div,
div:has(> .sidebar-group a[href='/user-guide/introduction']) + div {
  margin-top: 0 !important;
}

24-24z"></path></svg></div></a></div><span class="cursor-pointer">Prerequisites</span></h2>
<ul>
<li><strong>Node.js 24+</strong> — <a href="https://nodejs.org/" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer" class="link">Download</a></li>
<li><strong>Yarn 4</strong> — bundled with Node via Corepack. Enable it: <code>corepack enable</code></li>
<li><strong>Docker</strong> — <a href="https://www.docker.com/products/docker-desktop/" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer" class="link">Download</a>. Needed to run a local Twenty server. Skip if you already have Twenty running elsewhere.</li>
</ul>
<span data-as="p">Building a Twenty app has three phases. The scaffolder collapses them into one happy-path command, but each phase is a separate concept — when something fails, knowing which phase you’re in tells you what to fix.</span>
<div data-table-wrapper="true" class="[--page-padding:32px] overflow-x-auto flex w-[calc(100%+(var(--page-padding)*2))] my-[1em] py-[1em] -mx-[var(--page-padding)] max-w-none [contain:inline-size]"><div class="px-[var(--page-padding)] grow max-w-none table"><table class="m-0 min-w-full w-full max-w-none table [&_td]:min-w-[150px] [&_th]:text-left [&_td[data-numeric]]:tabular-nums"><thead><tr><th>Phase</th><th>What you do</th><th>Tool</th><th>Result</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><strong>1. Scaffold</strong></td><td>Generate the app’s source code</td><td><code>npx create-twenty-app</code></td><td>A TypeScript project on disk</td></tr><tr><td><strong>2. Run a server</strong></td><td>Start a Twenty server to sync into</td><td>Docker + <code>yarn twenty server</code></td><td>A running Twenty instance</td></tr><tr><td><strong>3. Sync</strong></td><td>Live-sync your code to the server</td><td><code>yarn twenty dev</code></td><td>Your changes appear in the UI</td></tr></tbody></table></div></div>

<span data-as="p">You’ll be prompted for a name and description — press <strong>Enter</strong> for the defaults. This generates a TypeScript project in <code>my-twenty-app/</code> with a starter <code>application-config.ts</code>, a default role, a CI workflow, and an integration test.</span>
<span data-as="p"><strong>After this phase:</strong> you have an app’s source code on your machine. It isn’t running yet — that’s Phase 2.</span>
<hr>
<h2 class="flex whitespace-pre-wrap group font-semibold" id="phase-2-—-run-a-local-twenty-server"><div class="absolute" tabindex="-1"><a href="#phase-2-—-run-a-local-twenty-server" class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link" aria-label="Navigate to header">​<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light"><svg aria-hidden="true" focusable="false" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="gray" height="12px" viewBox="0 0 576 512"><path d="M0 256C0 167.6 71.6 96 160 96h72c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H160C98.1 144 48 194.1 48 256s50.1 112 112 112h72c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H160C71.6 416 0 344.4 0 256zm576 0c0 88.4-71.6 160-160 160H344c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24h72c61.9 0 112-50.1 112-112s-50.1-112-112-112H344c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24h72c88.4 0 160 71.6 160 160zM184 232H392c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H184c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24z"></path></svg></div></a></div><span class="cursor-pointer">Phase 2 — Run a local Twenty server</span></h2>
<span data-as="p">Your app needs a Twenty server to sync into. The server is a full Twenty instance — UI, GraphQL API, PostgreSQL — running locally in Docker. Your local code uploads its definitions to that server, which makes them appear in the UI.</span>
<span data-as="p">The scaffolder offers to start one for you:</span>
<blockquote>
<span data-as="p"><strong>Would you like to set up a local Twenty instance?</strong></span>
</blockquote>
<ul>
<li><strong>Yes (recommended)</strong> — pulls the <code>twentycrm/twenty-app-dev</code> Docker image and starts it on port <code>2020</code>. Make sure Docker is running first.</li>
<li><strong>No</strong> — choose this if you already have a Twenty server you want to connect to. You can wire it up later with <code>yarn twenty remote:add</code>.</li>
</ul>

<span data-as="p"><strong>After this phase:</strong> you have a running Twenty server at <a href="http://localhost:2020" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer" class="link">http://localhost:2020</a> with your CLI authorized to sync to it.</span>
<div class="callout my-4 px-5 py-4 overflow-hidden rounded-2xl flex gap-3 border border-blue-200 bg-blue-50 dark:border-blue-900 dark:bg-blue-600/20" data-callout-type="note"><div class="mt-0.5 w-4" data-component-part="callout-icon"><svg width="14" height="14" viewBox="0 0 14 14" fill="currentColor" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" class="size-4 text-blue-800 dark:text-blue-300" aria-label="Note"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M7 1.3C10.14 1.3 12.7 3.86 12.7 7C12.7 10.14 10.14 12.7 7 12.7C5.48908 12.6974 4.0408 12.096 2.97241 11.0276C1.90403 9.9592 1.30264 8.51092 1.3 7C1.3 3.86 3.86 1.3 7 1.3ZM7 0C3.14 0 0 3.14 0 7C0 10.86 3.14 14 7 14C10.86 14 14 10.86 14 7C14 3.14 10.86 0 7 0ZM8 3H6V8H8V3ZM8 9H6V11H8V9Z"></path></svg></div><div class="text-sm prose dark:prose-invert min-w-0 w-full [&_kbd]:bg-background-light dark:[&_kbd]:bg-background-dark [&_code]:!text-current [&_kbd]:!text-current [&_a]:!text-current [&_a]:border-current [&_strong]:!text-current text-blue-800 dark:text-blue-300" data-component-part="callout-content"><span data-as="p">If Docker isn’t installed or running, the scaffolder will tell you the right start command for your OS. Once Docker is up, you can resume with <code>yarn twenty docker:start</code> — no need to re-scaffold.</span></div></div>

<span data-as="p">This watches <code>src/</code>, rebuilds on every change, and syncs the result to the server. Edit a file, save, and within a few seconds the server reflects the change. You’ll see a live status panel in your terminal.</span>
<span data-as="p">For more detailed output (build logs, sync requests, error traces), add <code>--verbose</code>.</span>

<span data-as="p">Click <strong>My twenty app</strong> to see its <strong>application registration</strong> — a server-level record describing your app (name, identifier, OAuth credentials, source). One registration can be installed across multiple workspaces on the same server.</span>

Runs until you stop it.</td><td>Interactive local development.</td></tr><tr><td><code>yarn twenty dev --once</code></td><td>Single build + sync, exits <code>0</code> on success, <code>1</code> on failure.</td><td>CI, pre-commit hooks, AI agents, scripted workflows.</td></tr></tbody></table></div></div>
<span data-as="p">Both modes need an authenticated remote.</span>
<h3 class="flex whitespace-pre-wrap group font-semibold" id="dev-mode-options"><div class="absolute" tabindex="-1"><a href="#dev-mode-options" class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link" aria-label="Navigate to header">​<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light"><svg aria-hidden="true" focusable="false" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="gray" height="12px" viewBox="0 0 576 512"><path d="M0 256C0 167.6 71.6 96 160 96h72c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H160C98.1 144 48 194.1 48 256s50.1 112 112 112h72c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H160C71.6 416 0 344.4 0 256zm576 0c0 88.4-71.6 160-160 160H344c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24h72c61.9 0 112-50.1 112-112s-50.1-112-112-112H344c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24h72c88.4 0 160 71.6 160 160zM184 232H392c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H184c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24z"></path></svg></div></a></div><span class="cursor-pointer">Dev mode options</span></h3>
<div data-table-wrapper="true" class="[--page-padding:32px] overflow-x-auto flex w-[calc(100%+(var(--page-padding)*2))] my-[1em] py-[1em] -mx-[var(--page-padding)] max-w-none [contain:inline-size]"><div class="px-[var(--page-padding)] grow max-w-none table"><table class="m-0 min-w-full w-full max-w-none table [&_td]:min-w-[150px] [&_th]:text-left [&_td[data-numeric]]:tabular-nums"><thead><tr><th>Flag</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><code>--once</code></td><td>Build and sync once, then exit.</td></tr><tr><td><code>--debounceMs <ms></code></td><td>Set the file-change debounce delay in milliseconds (default: <code>2000</code>).</td></tr><tr><td><code>--verbose</code> / <code>--debug</code></td><td>Show detailed build logs, sync requests, and error traces.</td></tr></tbody></table></div></div>
<h2 class="flex whitespace-pre-wrap group font-semibold" id="what-you-can-build"><div class="absolute" tabindex="-1"><a href="#what-you-can-build" class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link" aria-label="Navigate to header">​<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light"><svg aria-hidden="true" focusable="false" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="gray" height="12px" viewBox="0 0 576 512"><path d="M0 256C0 167.6 71.6 96 160 96h72c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H160C98.1 144 48 194.1 48 256s50.1 112 112 112h72c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H160C71.6 416 0 344.4 0 256zm576 0c0 88.4-71.6 160-160 160H344c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24h72c61.9 0 112-50.1 112-112s-50.1-112-112-112H344c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24h72c88.4 0 160 71.6 160 160zM184 232H392c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H184c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24z"></path></svg></div></a></div><span class="cursor-pointer">What you can build</span></h2>
<span data-as="p">Apps are composed of <strong>entities</strong> — each defined as a TypeScript file with a single <code>export default</code>:</span>
<div data-table-wrapper="true" class="[--page-padding:32px] overflow-x-auto flex w-[calc(100%+(var(--page-padding)*2))] my-[1em] py-[1em] -mx-[var(--page-padding)] max-w-none [contain:inline-size]"><div class="px-[var(--page-padding)] grow max-w-none table"><table class="m-0 min-w-full w-full max-w-none table [&_td]:min-w-[150px] [&_th]:text-left [&_td[data-numeric]]:tabular-nums"><thead><tr><th>Entity</th><th>What it does</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><strong>Objects & Fields</strong></td><td>Custom data models (Post Card, Invoice, etc.) with typed fields</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Logic functions</strong></td><td>Server-side TypeScript triggered by HTTP routes, cron schedules, or database events</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Front components</strong></td><td>React components that render inside Twenty’s UI (side panel, widgets, command menu)</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Skills & Agents</strong></td><td>AI capabilities — reusable instructions and autonomous assistants</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Views & Navigation</strong></td><td>Pre-configured list views and sidebar menu items</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Page layouts</strong></td><td>Custom record detail pages with tabs and widgets</td></tr></tbody></table></div></div>
<span data-as="p">Full reference: <a class="link" href="/developers/extend/apps/getting-started/concepts">Concepts</a>.</span>
